revision.comment: 刳抜式の石棺
revision.text:
'''割竹形石棺'''（わりたけがたせっかん）とは、古墳時代の棺の一つの種類をさす。
== 概要 == 本石棺は刳抜式の[[割竹形木棺]]を真似て造られたものであり、断面は円形で、両端を垂直に切ってある。そこに縄架け突起を付けたり、内部に石枕を造り出したりしてる。長さ2～3メートル。4世紀（古墳時代前期）に出現し、香川県などで造られた。その変容形が[[舟形石棺]]であり、それに先行して使用されたと推定されている。<ref>杉山晋作「割竹形石棺」/江坂輝彌他(2005) 432ページ</ref>
== 割竹形石棺の例 == *[[快天山古墳]]（香川県丸亀市綾歌町栗熊東に所在する前方後円墳）の3棺。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == *江坂輝彌・芹沢長介・坂詰秀一編『新日本考古学小辞典』ニュー・サイエンス社 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2
*永原慶二監修 石上英一他編集『岩波 日本史辞典』岩波書店 1999年 ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8
== 関連項目 == *[[古墳]] *[[棺]] *[[木棺]] *[[石棺墓]] *[[木棺墓]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:せつかん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:日本の考古遺物]] [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

'''割竹形石棺'''（わりたけがたせっかん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[棺]]の一種である。 [[画像:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|安福寺石棺（復元）
（一番奥、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]）]] == 概要 == 刳抜式の[[割竹形木棺]]を真似て造られたものであり、断面は円形で、両端を垂直に切ってある。そこに縄架け突起を付けたり、内部に石枕を造り出したりしている。長さは2～3mのものが多い。[[4世紀]]（[[古墳時代]]前期）に出現し、[[香川県]]などで造られた。その変容形が[[舟形石棺]]であり、それに先行して使用されたと推定されている。杉山晋作「割竹形石棺」/江坂輝彌他(2005) 432ページ == 割竹形石棺の例 == *[[快天山古墳]]（香川県丸亀市綾歌町栗熊東に所在する前方後円墳）の三棺。 *[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]所在石棺（大阪府柏原市、[[玉手山古墳群|玉手山3号墳]]から出土したと伝えられる） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *江坂輝彌・芹沢長介・坂詰秀一編『新日本考古学小辞典』ニュー・サイエンス社 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 *永原慶二監修　石上英一他編集『岩波　日本史辞典』岩波書店　1999年　ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == 関連項目 == *[[古墳]] *[[棺]] *[[木棺]] *[[弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|木棺墓]] {{DEFAULTSORT:わりたけかたせつかん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[割竹形石棺]] to [[Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins]]
revision.text: [[Category:Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins]][[ja:割竹形石棺]]

'''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenzan Kofun|ja|快天山古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfukuji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Coffin|ja|棺}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Archaeological features]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[Category:Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins]][[ja:割竹形石棺]]

'''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|ja|快天山古墳|12=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=draft}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=draft}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Coffin|ja|棺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Waritakegata Sekkan}}

{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|ja|快天山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Coffin|ja|棺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] }} [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{citation needed|Waritakegata Sekkan}}

(2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|ja|快天山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Coffin|ja|棺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] }} [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{citation needed|Waritakegata Sekkan}}

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:割竹形石棺 up to rev 52376962
revision.text:
'''割竹形石棺'''（わりたけがたせっかん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の[[棺]]の一種である。 [[画像:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|安福寺石棺（一番奥、復元）
[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]]] == 概要 == 刳抜式の[[割竹形木棺]]を真似て造られたものであり、断面は円形で、両端を垂直に切ってある。そこに縄架け突起を付けたり、内部に石枕を造り出したりしている。長さは2～3mのものが多い。[[4世紀]]（[[古墳時代]]前期）に出現し、[[香川県]]などで造られた。その変容形が[[舟形石棺]]であり、それに先行して使用されたと推定されている。杉山晋作「割竹形石棺」/江坂輝彌他(2005) 432ページ == 割竹形石棺の例 == *[[快天山古墳]]（香川県丸亀市綾歌町栗熊東に所在する前方後円墳）の三棺。 *[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]所在石棺（大阪府柏原市、[[玉手山古墳群|玉手山3号墳]]から出土したと伝えられる） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *江坂輝彌・芹沢長介・坂詰秀一編『新日本考古学小辞典』ニュー・サイエンス社 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 *永原慶二監修　石上英一他編集『岩波　日本史辞典』岩波書店　1999年　ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == 関連項目 == *[[古墳]] *[[棺]] *[[木棺]] *[[弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|石棺墓]] *[[弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|木棺墓]] {{DEFAULTSORT:わりたけかたせつかん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:遺構]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:棺]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
Bot: cleanup/reformat
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|ja|快天山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Coffin|ja|棺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|Waritakegata Sekkan}} {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] [[Category:Coffins to comment]][[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]][[Category:Coffins to comment]] }} [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]]

[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, revision.comment: Text replacement - "Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus" to "Chest-shaped stone coffin"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] }} [[sn:Waritakegata Sekkan]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sekkan]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sekkan|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sekkan|qq=}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] to [[Waritakegata Sarcophagus]]: Text replacement - "Waritakegata Sekkan" to "Waritakegata Sarcophagus"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped stone coffin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type stone coffin]] * [[Chest-shaped stone coffin]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped stone coffin) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A stone coffin located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|qq=|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] }} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] {{wikidata link|Q11398020}}

Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] }} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] {{wikidata link|Q11398020}}

[[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped Sarcophagus [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] }} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] {{wikidata link|Q11398020}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped Sarcophagus [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * revision.text:
[[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped Sarcophagus [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
[[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{wikidata link|Q11398020}} [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from [[:en:Kaitenyama Kofun|Kaitenyama Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * [[:en:Coffin|Coffin]] * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|割竹形石棺|version=52376962|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{wikidata link|Q11398020}} [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398020]] Wikidata name: split-bamboo-shaped Sarcophagus [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Need to find a consistent nomenclature for all of these. Sekkan and Mokka need some level of consistency because of how they are contrasted here * [[Funagata Mokkan]] * [[Funagata Sarcophagus]] * [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Box-type Sarcophagus]] * [[Chest-shaped Sarcophagus]] * [[Sekkan]] * [[Waritakegata Mokkan]] * [[Waritakegata Sekkan]] * [[Wooden Coffin]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|WD=Q184418|lt=Coffin}} from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|en|Kaitenyama Kofun|WD=Q11491385|lt=Kaitenyama Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} * {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|WD=Q184418|lt=Coffin}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Burials of the [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] [[ja:割竹形石棺]] '''Waritakegata Sekkan''' (割竹形石棺) is a type of {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|WD=Q184418|lt=Coffin|1=Coffin}} from the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Asuka Museum Sekkan.jpg|250px|thumb|Anfukuji Stone Coffin (rearmost, reconstructed)
{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}]] == Overview == It was made in imitation of the carved-out {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, with a circular cross-section and vertically cut ends. Rope suspension projections were sometimes attached, and stone pillows were crafted inside. Most examples measure between 2 and 3 meters in length. They first appeared in the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} (early [[Kofun period]]) and were produced in areas such as {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Funagata Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (boat-shaped Sarcophagus) is considered a variant, and the waritakegata sekkan is thought to have been used prior to it.Sugiyama Shinsaku, "Waritakegata Sekkan" / Esaka Teruya et al. (2005), p. 432 == Examples of Waritakegata Sekkan == * Three coffins from {{ill|Kaitenyama Kofun|en|Kaitenyama Kofun|WD=Q11491385|lt=Kaitenyama Kofun|1=Kaitenyama Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in Kurikuma Higashi, Ayauta Town, Marugame City, Kagawa Prefecture. * A Sarcophagus located at {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, said to have been excavated from {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Tamateyama Mound No. 3 in Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture. == Notes == == References == * Esaka Teruya, Serizawa Chosuke, Sakazume Shuichi (eds.), *Shin Nihon Kōkogaku Ko Jiten*, New Science Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Supervised by Nagahara Keiji, edited by Ishigami Eiichi et al., *Iwanami Nihonshi Jiten*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999, ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|WD=Q184418|lt=Coffin|1=Coffin}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|ja|弥生時代の墓制#石棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Burials of the Yayoi Period|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} *
